The '''second person''' is one of the four [[grammatical persons]] in Aelaki. It refers to the addressee.

== Ki Syllables ==

Second person [[Ki]] syllables vary by [[gender]]:

{| class="wikitable"
! Gender !! Ki Syllable !! Word-Final Form
|-
| Child || ju || -uj
|-
| Female || jo || -oj
|-
| Male || ja || -aj
|}

== Usage ==

Like all persons in Aelaki, the second person is marked through [[Ki]] affixes rather than through standalone pronouns, which have disappeared from the language. When additional pragmatic nuance is needed, [[pseudopronouns]] — full nouns inflected with Ki suffixes — serve this function.

Several pseudopronouns that involve a [[first person]] speaker addressing a second person participant exist, such as ''thijith'' ('you + me, alone'), ''thishith'' ('you + me, few others'), and ''thikith'' ('you + me, many others').

== Verb Agreement ==

In verb agreement, second person Ki markers appear in the following positions:

* '''Transitive verbs''': prefix Ki (subject) and suffix Ki (object)
* '''Intransitive active verbs''': prefix Ki (subject)
* '''Intransitive stative verbs''': suffix Ki (subject)

== See Also ==

* [[First person]]
* [[Third person]]
* [[Fourth person]]
* [[Ki]]
* [[Pseudopronouns]]

[[Category:grammatical persons]]
